page: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Eadsv7 server family. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with x86_64 architecture, it features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per vCPU. It also includes 3780 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not bundle any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core capabilities. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression benchmarks all rank in the top 10%. Multi-threaded performance is also robust, with the passmark CPU score and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression ranking in the top 25%. Memory performance is solid, with a passmark memory score in the top 25%, while uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths sit in the average tier.

For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in static web serving and small-model LLM text generation, both scoring in the top tier or top 25%. However, it exhibits a clear tradeoff in large-scale AI tasks; while prompt processing for a 70B LLM model is average, text generation for the same model falls into the bottom 10%. This highlights a hardware profile optimized for memory capacity and single-core throughput rather than heavy parallelized tensor computations.

Given its high memory density and fast local storage, this server is highly cost-efficient for memory-bound enterprise applications. It is ideally suited for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, data compression pipelines, and static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eadsv7 family, featuring an AMD EPYC 9V45 x86_64 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a 4.5 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, delivering a high 16 GB per vCPU ratio, alongside 3780 GB of local storage. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and static web serving throughput. However, without GPU acceleration, it exhibits poor performance on large-scale LLM text generation. This server offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, making it highly suitable for large databases, in-memory caching, and data-processing pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases and caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, built for databases and caching.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high-capacity local storage and top-tier single-core performance for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 4.5 GHz clock speed
512 GB of RAM providing a high 16 GB per vCPU memory ratio
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Best suited for large databases, in-memory caching, and data pipelines
